Title: The miR3367–lncRNA67–GhCYP724B module regulates male sterility by modulating brassinosteroid biosynthesis and interacting with Aorf27 in Gossypium hirsutum

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates brassinosteroid accumulation and pollen fertility; loss causes BR-deficiency symptoms, male sterility, and dwarfism.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flower buds, especially in fertile maintainer line 2074B; not expressed or much lower in CMS line 2074A.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Flower bud-specific; enriched from pollen mother cell to late uninucleate pollen stages in fertile plants.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Long non-coding RNA; endogenous target mimic (eTM)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (transient expression constructs in Nicotiana benthamiana)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ssRNA-seq; qRT-PCR; microscale thermophoresis; transient co-expression assay with GFP reporter; VIGS silencing; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field growth; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; VIGS in cotton
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: lncRNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA1167799; transcriptome RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA1166841
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for normal pollen development, tapetum/microspore development, and plant height regulation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout causes dwarfism and severe male sterility.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a target for manipulating cytoplasmic male sterility and hybrid seed production in cotton, but disruption may induce dwarfism and sterility.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of GhCYP724B by sequestering miR3367 rather than encoding a protein; central non-coding component of the BR-linked CMS module.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lncRNA67 is a flower-bud-specific lncRNA highly expressed in fertile cotton lines. 2. It acts as an endogenous target mimic of miR3367, protecting GhCYP724B transcripts from cleavage. 3. Silencing or knockout of lncRNA67 lowers BR content and causes dwarfism and male sterility in cotton.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a CMS-associated toxic factor; ectopic expression causes male sterility in tobacco and inhibits yeast growth.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flower buds of sterile line 2074A and hybrid F1-A; nearly undetectable in maintainer line 2074B; barely expressed in TM-1 leaves, seeds, or fibers.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in tobacco overexpression construct (cox4-Aorf27)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; semi-quantitative PCR; transgenic overexpression in tobacco; pollen staining and germination assays; yeast growth assay; LCI; BiFC; yeast two-hybrid
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated tobacco transformation; yeast heterologous expression
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduces pollen viability, pollen germination, pod size, seed number, and seed weight; toxic to yeast cells.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential mitochondrial CMS factor for creating or diagnosing male sterility systems, but strong deleterious effects require controlled deployment.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a classical metabolic enzyme or TF; contributes to CMS partly through interaction with GhCYP724B and is negatively associated with GhCYP724B abundance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Aorf27 is a unique mitochondrial ORF highly expressed in the sterile cotton line 2074A. 2. Ectopic expression in tobacco causes male sterility and poor seed set, while expression in yeast is toxic. 3. Aorf27 physically interacts with GhCYP724B and is proposed to cooperate with BR deficiency to promote CMS.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a 22α-hydroxylation step in BR biosynthesis, contributing to formation of downstream BR intermediates and castasterone.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: BR sterol intermediates upstream of typhasterol/castasterone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 22-hydroxylated BR intermediates leading to TY and CS
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor pool for active BR production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting/key control step supported by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes BR accumulation and pollen fertility; silencing/knockout reduces BR intermediates, causes BR-deficiency symptoms, dwarfism, and male sterility/semi-sterility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flower buds of fertile lines; positively correlated with lncRNA67.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Flower bud-enriched; reduced in lncRNA67-silenced plants and CMS line 2074A.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5'-RACE for miRNA cleavage; VIGS silencing; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; BR metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/MS; subcellular localization; BiFC; LCI; yeast two-hybrid; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and field validation; transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; heterologous silencing in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transient GFP reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls tapetum and microspore development, male fertility, and normal plant architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA1166841; lncRNA association from PRJNA1167799
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes dwarfism and strong male sterility; reduced seed set in tobacco homolog knockdown.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering CMS and BR-related plant architecture in cotton; useful in hybrid breeding but must avoid excessive BR deficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a target of miR3367 and positively regulated by lncRNA67 acting as an eTM; homologous to Arabidopsis DWF4 according to the paper.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCYP724B is the central BR-biosynthetic enzyme experimentally linked to male fertility in cotton. 2. Silencing or knockout decreases BR intermediates (28-homoCS, 28-norCS, 6-deoxoCS, TY, CS), downregulates BR biosynthesis/signaling genes, and causes dwarf sterile plants. 3. GhCYP724B physically interacts with GhDIM, GhCYP90B, and the CMS-associated mitochondrial ORF Aorf27.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of 24-methylenecholesterol to campesterol, supplying sterol precursor for BR biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target BR production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively supports pollen fertility; silencing causes sterility or semi-sterility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in VIGS plants; functional in flower buds/anthers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-localization; BiFC; VIGS silencing; pollen viability and germination assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VIGS in cotton; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports normal pollen development and fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduces pollen viability and anther fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate partner gene for stacking with BR-pathway genes, but suppression risks fertility loss.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway gene because the Results section functionally tests it by VIGS and protein interaction with GhCYP724B.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhDIM is a BR-biosynthetic precursor-supply enzyme that physically interacts with GhCYP724B at the plasma membrane. 2. Silencing GhDIM in cotton causes sterility or semi-sterility and lowers pollen performance. 3. The study supports GhDIM as a positive component of the GhCYP724B-centered BR fertility module.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a BR-biosynthetic cytochrome P450 contributing to steroid hydroxylation steps required for BR accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies intermediate pool for target BR production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively supports pollen fertility; silencing causes sterility or semi-sterility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in VIGS plants; active in flower buds/anthers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-localization; BiFC; VIGS silencing; pollen viability and germination assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: VIGS in cotton; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing impairs pollen viability and fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering partner with GhCYP724B for BR manipulation, but suppression will compromise fertility.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway gene because it was experimentally silenced and shown to affect fertility in the Results.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCYP90B physically interacts with GhCYP724B and co-localizes at the plasma membrane. 2. Silencing GhCYP90B in cotton reduces pollen viability/germination and causes sterility or semi-sterility. 3. The data place GhCYP90B as a positive BR-biosynthetic component in cotton pollen fertility.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Brassinosteroid biosynthesis via control of GhCYP724B abundance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of BR biosynthesis module; increased miR3367 activity reduces GhCYP724B and is associated with male sterility.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative controller of BR production through direct targeting of the key enzyme GhCYP724B
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in flower buds of 2074A and 2074B; levels higher in lncRNA67 knockout mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Flower buds; elevated in lncRNA67 mutants LM04 and LM13.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Sequestered by lncRNA67 endogenous target mimic
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Target prediction; 5'-RACE; microscale thermophoresis with lncRNA67; transient co-expression GFP assay; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports normal pollen development when GhCYP724B repression is relieved.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess repression of GhCYP724B is expected to cause BR deficiency, dwarfism, and male sterility.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning CMS through miRNA mimicry/editing of target sites or eTM deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as a newly detected novel miRNA in cotton; core regulator because direct target cleavage and phenotypic module were experimentally validated.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR3367 directly cleaves GhCYP724B transcripts in vivo. 2. lncRNA67 binds miR3367 and blocks this repression, thereby sustaining BR biosynthesis. 3. Increased miR3367 activity is linked to reduced GhCYP724B expression and male sterility.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation downstream of BR signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes involved in tapetum and microspore development containing E-box and BRRE motifs in their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely activates or modulates downstream pollen-development genes in response to BR signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: BR signal transduction control of pollen-development gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Implicated as downstream BR-signaling regulator linking BR status to male fertility gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key downstream BR-signaling integrator, but not directly genetically manipulated in this study
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: GhBES1 expression correlated with lncRNA67 in network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: BR signaling genes including BES1/BZR1-associated components were downregulated in GhCYP724B mutants
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression/network analysis; promoter cis-element analysis of downstream DEGs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls transcriptional response required for tapetum and microspore development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downstream signaling node potentially useful for fertility regulation, but evidence here is indirect compared with GhCYP724B.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included cautiously as a core regulatory gene because the Results focus on BES1/BZR1-mediated downstream control of pollen-development genes, though no direct mutant/transgenic validation was provided in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BES1/BZR1 is proposed as the key BR-signaling TF connecting reduced BR biosynthesis to altered pollen-development gene expression. 2. Many DEGs in sterile mutants carry BES1/BZR1-binding motifs in their promoters. 3. The study suggests BR-dependent BES1/BZR1 regulation as the downstream transcriptional mechanism of fertility control.
